IDM.project_ai_summary:
The service does not currently meet Service Standard 14 (operating a reliable service). Of the 17 repositories assessed, 11 have passed their Quality Gates, while 5 have failed and one has not yet been assessed. A number of high-severity issues are present, including 16 major bugs and 43 major vulnerabilities, along with a high number of code smells (22 critical, 689 major, and 218 minor).

Defra Development standards are not consistently met. Seven repositories are below the 90% test coverage target, and four exceed the 3% code duplication limit. These findings indicate gaps in code quality and testing coverage.

From a governance perspective, there are seven inactive projects, four stale branches, 25 false positives recorded without justification, and 136 suppressions without justification comments. These issues affect visibility and the accuracy of reporting.
IDM.path_to_green: Resolve the 16 major bugs and 43 major vulnerabilities as a priority, then work through the critical and major code smells to reduce technical debt; Improve test coverage in the 7 repositories below 90% and reduce duplication in the 4 repositories above 3%; Review the 7 inactive projects, remove the 4 stale branches, and add justification comments to the 25 false positives and 136 suppressions to restore visibility.

SFI.project_ai_summary:
The service shows good code quality across its 5 repositories, with no bugs or vulnerabilities identified and only a small number of code smells present. Of the 5 repositories assessed, 4 have passed their Quality Gates, while one has failed, indicating some risk to reliability. The service largely aligns with Service Standard 14 (operating a reliable service).

All repositories meet Defra Development standards, maintaining 95.2% test coverage and 0.2% code duplication.

From a governance perspective, one false positive and one suppression have been recorded without justification comments, which are minor hygiene items worth addressing for auditability.
SFI.path_to_green: Investigate and resolve the Quality Gate failure in the one affected repository; Add justification comments to the one false positive and one suppression to maintain auditability.
